feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Restricting Journal Entry Reversals
entry.content:
A common requirement is to allow users to display, edit, or post journal entries while preventing them from reversing journal entries.

With the introduction of App Authorization Variants (AAVs), this can now be achieved through standard role configuration without using UI Adaptation.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: CDS view to get the PO Workflow data
entry.content:
Currently we are using I_WorkflowStatusOverview CDS to retrieve the information but only Ready , Cancelled and Completed Status tasks are displayed. Could you please help us with a CDS view which retrieves the entire information especially the steps that have  'planned'status


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Create Kanban Control Cycles with Predefined External Container IDs
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a question regarding Kanban in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Our customer already has several thousand physical Kanban containers in use. Each container is labeled with a unique external container ID that is physically attached to the container and should continue to be used after the SAP implementation.

Question:

Is there any standard functionality that allows creating Kanban control cycles while assigning predefined external container IDs to the individual Kanban containers?

Ideally, we would like to:

  • Create the Kanban control cycle.
  • Assign existing external container IDs to the Kanban containers during creation or via a mass upload.
  • Avoid SAP generating new container IDs, as the existing physical labels should remain unchanged.

Has anyone implemented a similar scenario, or is there a recommended best practice (e.g. migration object, API, configuration, or enhancement option) to map existing physical Kanban container IDs to SAP Kanban containers?

Any guidance or experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Move SB 2SL to CBC 3SL Asks
entry.content:
1. Mixed purchase invoice docking:
Currently, the standard 2SL interface cannot place Goods Issue and Goods Return on the same invoice. Can 3SL be solved by custom development or other solutions? Hope SAP expert to share a solution or the logic/ideas.
混合采购发票对接：
目前 2SL的标准接口无法将“发货”和“退货”放在同一张发票上。请问 3SL能否通过自定义开发或其他方案解决？希望分享方案或实现思路。

2. Interface creation process:  
What is the difference for 3SL vs 2SL regarding the creation process of custom and standard interfaces? We hope that the interface development process in 3SL can be demonstrated via a video sharing.
接口创建流程：
自定义接口和标准接口的创建流程 3SLvs 2SL有什么不同？希望有演示视频分享。

3. Interface Extensibility Benefits:
Where exactly is stronger for 3SL vs 2SL in API interface expansion? We would like to give you an example of what 2SL can’t do.
接口拓展性优势：
在API接口拓展性上3SL vs 2SL具体强在哪里？希望能举例说明它可以实现哪些 2SL做不到的功能。


4. Migration of nearly 100 existing interfaces:
We are currently using nearly 100 custom and standard interfaces (attached) for 2SL, can these old interfaces be smooth compatible after upgrading to 3 SL, or do we need to rewrite them all?
现有近 100 个接口的迁移：
我们目前在用的自定义和标准接口将近 100 个（参见附件），2SL升级 3SL后这些旧接口能平滑兼容吗，还是需要全部重构重写？

5. Operations vs. UX Differences: What is the clear difference for 3SL vs 2SL regarding IT daily operations management and the business user interface, usage habits?
运维与用户体验差异：在 IT 日常运维管理以及业务用户的操作界面、使用习惯上，3SL vs2 SL有哪些明显的区别？

entry.title: How to match Intercompany Stock Transfer , COGS & Revenue posting in Intercompany Matching ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a question regarding Intercompany Matching and Reconciliation (ICMR) for intercompany stock transfer scenarios.

For stock transfer transactions, the sending company posts:

  • COGS at the time of outbound delivery.

  • Intercompany Revenue when the corresponding intercompany billing document is created.

Since both postings reside in the sending company (leading unit), how should these P&L postings be handled in ICMR? Is it recommended to:

  • Configure a dedicated matching method for stock transfers (e.g., based on GL accounts and Billing Type = CBST), or

  • Use another standard approach for matching these documents?

Additionally, we have a standard intercompany sales scenario where:

  • IC Revenue and IC Receivable from the intercompany billing document in the sending company are matched with

  • IC COGS (599*) and IC Payable from the supplier invoice in the receiving company.

Would it be a good practice to combine both intercompany sales and intercompany stock transfer P&L reconciliation into a single matching method, or should they be maintained separately?

I would appreciate any recommendations or best practices from those who have implemented ICMR for these scenarios.

Thanks,
Sagar

entry.title: monitor material coverage does not display supplier
entry.content:
Dear sap,

When we use app-monitor material coverage, it does not display supplier for some external materials.

For example, material JH5104000010  does not display supplier, But material JH5104000030 is OK.

But when we use app-Stock/Requirements List(MD04), it could display supplier with the material JH5104000010 .

And we find the only difference between these two materials is 

JH5104000030 have PO, but JH5104000010 only have PR. 

Is this the reason for the inconsistency in display between the two materials in the -monitor material coverage？

Thanks 

Ying

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud | Business Roles for User Management and Role Management
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm setting up the Authorization concept for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition system. My plan is to create 2 separate business roles:

  • One role for User Management
  • One role for Role Management

Question: 

  • Is this approach reasonable?
  • I'm not yet sure which business catalogs and apps I should assign to each role. Could you please recommend the correct catalogs/apps for each of these two roles?

Thank you very much for your advice.

Best regards,
QuocDT5

entry.title: Base unit and order unit not compatible error in SAP
entry.content:
Could you please confirm whether UoM conversion from EA to Bottle (or any other required UoM) is expected to be supported for free-text items without a Material Master in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Currently, when creating a Purchase Order against a Purchase Contract with UoM EA and changing the PO UoM to Bottle, the system returns the error "Base Unit and Order Unit are not compatible."

Could you advise the recommended approach for this scenario and whether any standard configuration is available to support such UoM conversions for free-text procurement.

entry.title: Bulk Upload of quality Inspection Results in SAP
entry.content:
We need a way to upload inspection results for serialized materials in bulk in SAP.
The standard "Record Inspection Results in Table form" app allows users to record inspection results, but it does not support uploading results for multiple inspection lots from an Excel file. Therefore, users must enter the inspection results manually for each inspection lot.
Each inspection lot contains 20 to 50 serial numbers. Even when the inspection result is the same for all serial numbers, users must enter the same result separately for each serial number. This makes the process slow and time-consuming.
Is there a standard SAP solution to upload inspection results in bulk from an Excel file? If not, is custom development required? If custom development is needed, please provide the recommended approach and the step-by-step process.

entry.title: System-Defined Breakdown Category ID Not Transported to the Test System
entry.content: The BDC §0SH (Statistical Shares) is a system-defined BDC that is available in the H0R/100 customizing tenant. However, we could not find the same BDC in the HS7/100 test tenant. Is there any additional configuration is required?

entry.title: How can users trace the Address Number displayed in the Purchase Requisition?
entry.content:
How can users trace the Address Number displayed in the Purchase Requisition back to the originating Supplier/Customer Business Partner record?  

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 - Introduction of Configurable Depreciation Profiles
entry.content:
Dear Team,We are reviewing the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 innovation "Introduction of Configurable Depreciation Profiles" and require clarification regarding its impact on customers operating in a 2-System Landscape (2SL).Based on SAP documentation, we understand that:

  • During the 2608 upgrade, depreciation keys are automatically converted to depreciation profiles for all customers.
  • Asset master records are automatically assigned the corresponding depreciation profiles.
  • Only pilot customers can actively use and configure depreciation profiles.
  • No change in depreciation calculation is expected after the conversion.

We would appreciate clarification on the following points specifically for 2SL :

  1. Is the automatic conversion from depreciation keys to depreciation profiles applicable to all 2SL customers during the 2608 upgrade?
  2. Will any customer validation or post-upgrade activities be required to ensure correct assignment of depreciation profiles to existing asset master records?
  3. For customers using SAP-delivered depreciation keys and/or expert-configured depr

entry.title: SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF - Workflow parameter input field disappears when property is assigned
entry.content:
HI Team,

Thank you for your suggestion. I implemented the solution exactly as recommended using both extension points:

  • SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF
  • SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL

The custom workflow condition is successfully displayed in Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions.

However, I am facing an issue while trying to provide F4 Value Help for the custom parameter.

What I have tested

1. Without OData configuration

Parameter contains only:

  • id
  • name
  • shorttext
  • xsd_type
  • mandatory
  • ddic_type

Result

  • Input field is displayed.
  • No F4 help is available

2. Using a Custom CDS View (Scenario = Value Help)

I created a custom CDS View based on I_PurchaseRequisitionType and published it successfully.

Configured in SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF:

service_path
entity
property

Result

  • The parameter input field disappears completely.

3. Using SAP Standard Workflow Value Help

Instead of the custom CDS, I used the standard workflow service:

  • Service: S_MMPURWORKFLOWVH_CDS
  • EntitySet: C_PurReqnTypeForWorkflow
  • Property: PurchasingDocumentType

I verified from the $metadata that:

  • Service exists
  • EntitySet exists
  • Property exists

However, after assigning the property in the parameter definition, the workflow UI no longer displays the input field.

Summary of my observations

Configuration Result
id + name + mandatory Input field displayed
+ ddic_typeInput field displayed
+ service_pathInput field displayed
+ entity Input field displayed
+ propertyInput field disappears completely

It appears that the Workflow framework rejects the parameter once the property is specified.

My Question

Has anyone successfully implemented an F4 Value Help for a custom workflow condition parameter in SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF?

If yes, could you please share:

  1. Whether a standard or custom OData service was used?
  2. Does the property require additional ValueList/Common.ValueList annotations?
  3. Is F4 Help currently supported for custom workflow condition parameters in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, or is this a framework limitation?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Missing search and selection filters in the Physical Inventory Data Entry app
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We would like to request clarification regarding the search and selection capabilities available in the Physical Inventory Data Entry application in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.Our business users have identified that certain search and filter fields required for their operational process are not available in the standard application for example in MRP controller field. We have reviewed the available configuration options but could not find any SSCUI or standard configuration that allows these search and selection filters to be added or enhanced.Could you please confirm the following:Is it possible to add or extend the search and selection filters in the standard Physical Inventory Data Entry app?If this functionality is not supported, is there any recommended standard approach, extensibility option, or roadmap item that addresses this requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?Any guidance or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support.

Regards,

entry.title: Preventing returned used equipment from distorting moving average price
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Scenario

We rent serialized devices (POS terminals / tablets) to customers. Devices are purchased into stock and valuated at moving average price. During the rental period they are capitalized and depreciated. When the rental contract ends, the device is physically returned to our warehouse and needs to be taken back into stock so it can either be re-rented or sold as used.

The problem

A returned device comes back at a value materially below the current moving average price of that material. A device that spent two years on rent might return at roughly 77% of original cost — the next one might return at 90%, or at 40%, depending entirely on how long it was out.

If those returns are received into the same valuation pool as new devices, they drag down the moving average price of stock that has never left the warehouse. Costing and margin reporting on new-device sales then carries wear that has nothing to do with those units. The same issue applies within the used pool itself, where returns arrive at very different values.

We already treat new and used devices as separate product categories commercially. What we have not settled is how that distinction should be represented in material valuation.

Questions

  1. What is the recommended way in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to hold depreciated/returned stock in a separate valuation pool from new stock of the same physical product?
  2. Is there a standard approach for materials whose value depends on condition or age rather than being uniform across the stock?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Storage Bin Field not available in Material Document BAPI - i_materialdocumenttp
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

We have received a requirement from our client to facilitate the transfer of stock in bulk for Warehouse Management (WM Storage Location). Specifically, we need to transfer unrestricted stock to sale order stock, sales order stock to unrestricted stock, and sales order to sales order stock. However, upon reviewing the standard functionality available in the system, we have found that it does not exist.

To address this requirement, we have developed a customized application utilizing the BAPI - "i_materialdocumenttp." However, we encountered an issue as we could not find the Storage Bin field within this BAPI.

Could you please guide us on how we can access this field or obtain the necessary information to fulfill this requirement?

Regards,

Sudhir Singh

entry.title: Prevent Outbound Delivery deletion in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, Experts!

I have a business requirement to prevent deletion of outbound deliveries based on the value of a custom field. (in short, we want to prevent deletion of documents that have reached our 3pl warehouse). 

My hope was to use the BAdi  "LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK" as it has access to the custom fields, but the issue I am having is that there is no separate 'documentprocessingmode' for deletion (Deletion uses "change"), and so I do not know how to distinguish between a normal change of the delivery and a deletion. 

Any tips?

Best regards,

Kristian

entry.title: Index Linked Bond - Security valuation - Book value
entry.content:
How to exclude Security valuations from the book value of TPM1 valuations.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Restricting Users to View Only Their Own PR & PO
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are working on a business scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition where end users create Purchase Requisitions (PRs).

In our business process, the user who creates the Purchase Requisition is different from the purchasing user who processes the PR and creates the Purchase Order (PO).

Therefore, we would like to restrict end users so that they can only view the Purchase Requisitions they created and the related Purchase Orders created as a result of their own PRs.

For example:

  • User A → should only be able to view PRs created by User A.
  • User B → should only be able to view PRs created by User B.
  • User A should not be able to view User B's PRs.
  • Once the PR is approved and a related PO is created, User A should only be able to track the PO / purchasing process related to their own PR.
  • User A should not be able to view the POs or order tracking information created as a result of other users' approved PRs.
  • Purchasing users should still be able to view and process all PRs and POs within their assigned authorization scope.

Our main requirement is to prevent end users from accessing each other's purchasing process data. Therefore, we believe that the visibility of both Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders should be restricted based on the user who initiated the purchasing process.

We would like to understand the following:

  1. Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, using standard authorization capabilities, to restrict a user so that they can only view Purchase Requisitions created by themselves?
  2. Is it possible to restrict Purchase Requisitions in Manage Purchase Requisitions or related PR applications based on the Requester / Created By user?
  3. Once a PR is approved and a PO is created, is it possible to restrict the user so that they can only view and track the PO related to their own PR?
  4. Is there a standard authorization or restriction available in My Purchase Orders or related purchase order tracking applications that allows users to view only the POs related to their own Purchase Requisitions?
  5. Is there a specific Restriction Type / Access Category or Business Role authorization that can be used to restrict both PR and PO visibility based on the Requester / Created By user?
  6. What is the standard SAP-recommended approach to allow purchasing users to access all relevant PRs and POs while restricting end users to only their own purchasing processes?
  7. Has anyone implemented a similar scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? If so, could you please share the authorization design or solution approach you used?

We are specifically looking for a standard solution that prevents end users from viewing each other's Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders, while still allowing purchasing users to access and process all relevant documents within their authorization scope.

Thank you in advance for your help.

BR,
Evrim
@MerveBayturk @Melis_Sorgun @rodoplu_umut

entry.title: SAP S4Hana Public Cloud Edition - Query Browser Links not working
entry.content:
After a recent update, SAP S4Hana Public Cloud Edition - Query Browser Links do not appear to work. I get the following Error message when I click "Open for Analysis" after selecting an Analytical Query:

"Error
Sorry, the app couldn't be opened.
The navigation target "#AnalyticQuery-preview?query=2CCAPFLXBLAGING&queryType=query&sap-app-origin-hint=" of the app could not be resolved. This can be caused, for example, by an incorrect content configuration, an incorrect or missing role assignment, or because your current device is not supported.".

Title: ErrorMessage: Sorry, the app couldn't be opened.Details: The navigation target "#AnalyticQuery-preview?query=2CCAPFLXBLAGING&queryType=query&sap-app-origin-hint=" of the app could not be resolved. This can be caused, for example, by an incorrect content configuration, an incorrect or missing role assignment, or because your current device is not supported.

This is happening no matter the query in Query Browser.
